questions.original_question: Tell me more about Lotus management team
questions.answer:
The management team of Lotus Technology includes several key leaders who bring extensive experience and expertise to the company. Mr. Qingfeng Feng serves as the Chief Executive Officer, leading the company with his visionary approach and over 20 years of experience at Geely Holding. Mr. Daxue Wang was appointed as the Chief Financial Officer on April 8, 2025, bringing a wealth of financial and operational experience from his previous roles at Radar Auto and other prominent institutions. Mr. Alexious Lee, who previously held the CFO position, remains a member of the board of directors. Additionally, Ms. Catherine Cai joined as an independent director on March 31, 2025, bringing over three decades of investment banking expertise to the board. These leaders collectively drive Lotus Technology’s mission to innovate and excel in the luxury mobility sector.

This information is based on knowledge available before 2025-04-08.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology has a pioneering, tech-forward, and design-led management team with expertise in automotive, technology, and innovation. Led by Mr. Qingfeng Feng, an industry veteran and a visionary leader who has served at Geely Holding for more than 20 years, Lotus Technology's management team is made up of seasoned professionals with diversified backgrounds in R&D, technology, and automotive design, and with extensive industry experience at other leading automotive firms, such as Geely Holding, Volvo, Mercedes Benz, BMW, Porsche, Maserati, Aston Martin, Ferrari, and Bugatti.

rag_info.chunk_content: NEW YORK, April 08, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced changes to Lotus Technology's management team and board of directors. The board of directors of Lotus Technology Inc. (the “Board”) has approved the appointment of Mr. Daxue Wang as the Chief Financial Officer (“CFO”) of Lotus Technology Inc. effective April 8, 2025. Mr. Wang is a seasoned executive across corporate and prominent financial institutions, most recently serving as CFO of Radar Auto. Prior to that, Mr. Wang held several executive positions in Zhejiang Kangsheng, Huzhou Moshan Asset Management, Morgan Stanley Securities, ICBC Credit Suisse Asset Management, and Citi Group. The Board believes that Mr. Wang is highly qualified and a suitable candidate. The appointment came after Mr. Alexious Lee tendered his resignation as CFO of Lotus Technology Inc. for personal reasons on April 3, 2025. Mr. Lee will remain as a member of the board of directors. As previously announced on its filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, Lotus Technology Inc. has also appointed Ms. Catherine Cai as an independent director as well as a member of the audit committee, the compensation committee, and the nominating and corporate governance committee of Lotus Technology's board of directors, with effect from March 31, 2025. Ms. Catherine Cai has more than three decades of experience in China's investment banking industry and has held senior positions at international investment banks.
rag_info.sub_query: Who are the members of Lotus Technology's management team?

rag_info.chunk_content: She was Group Managing Director, Global Executive Vice Chairman, and Chairman of Greater China at UBS. She also held multiple senior positions at Citigroup and Bank of America Merrill Lynch. Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc., said: “Lotus Technology is delighted to welcome Mr. Daxue Wang as the new Chief Financial Officer. Mr. Wang brings years of financial and operational experience with a proven track record for driving financial excellence. I would also like to thank Mr. Alexious Lee for his contributions as Chief Financial Officer and look forward to his continued support as a board member.” Mr. Donghui Li, Chairman of Lotus Technology Inc.'s board of directors, commented: “Lotus Technology is honored to welcome Ms. Catherine Cai to its board. Her deep expertise in investment banking will bring significant value to the shareholders as Lotus Technology continues to execute its long-term vision.”

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has a pioneering, tech-forward, and design-led management team with expertise in automotive, technology, and innovation. Led by Mr. Qingfeng Feng, an industry veteran and a visionary leader who has served at Geely Holding for more than 20 years, Lotus Technology's management team is made up of seasoned professionals with diversified backgrounds in R&D, technology, and automotive design, and with extensive industry experience at other leading automotive firms, such as Geely Holding, Volvo, Mercedes Benz, BMW, Porsche, Maserati, Aston Martin, Ferrari, and Bugatti.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Strengths 
summary: The company is strategically positioned as a pioneer in the luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, benefiting from its iconic brand heritage and advanced technological capabilities. With over 70 years of experience in sports car design and engineering, the brand is recognized for its commitment to aerodynamics and lightweight design, complemented by a successful motorsport legacy that includes multiple Formula 1 championships. 

Leveraging proprietary next-generation technologies developed through robust research and development, the company aims to offer a fully electric product portfolio, targeting the underserved luxury BEV segment with models priced between $80,000 and $149,000. The company plans to transition to a 100% BEV lineup by 2027, establishing a competitive edge in electrification.

Supported by Geely Holding's extensive resources, the company operates an asset-light business model that enhances operational efficiency and enables rapid global expansion. This partnership provides access to advanced manufacturing facilities and a reliable supply chain, ensuring stability in sourcing critical components.

The company is also committed to sustainability, adhering to high international ESG standards and aiming for a complete lineup of electric vehicles. Its digital-first, omni-channel sales model enhances the luxury retail experience, integrating online and physical services to meet customer needs effectively.

Guided by a visionary leadership team with extensive experience in the automotive industry, the company is well-positioned to maintain its competitive advantages and drive innovation in the luxury electric vehicle market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: NEW YORK, April 08, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced changes to Lotus Technology's management team and board of directors. The board of directors of Lotus Technology Inc. (the “Board”) has approved the appointment of Mr. Daxue Wang as the Chief Financial Officer (“CFO”) of Lotus Technology Inc. effective April 8, 2025. Mr. Wang is a seasoned executive across corporate and prominent financial institutions, most recently serving as CFO of Radar Auto. Prior to that, Mr. Wang held several executive positions in Zhejiang Kangsheng, Huzhou Moshan Asset Management, Morgan Stanley Securities, ICBC Credit Suisse Asset Management, and Citi Group. The Board believes that Mr. Wang is highly qualified and a suitable candidate. The appointment came after Mr. Alexious Lee tendered his resignation as CFO of Lotus Technology Inc. for personal reasons on April 3, 2025. Mr. Lee will remain as a member of the board of directors. As previously announced on its filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, Lotus Technology Inc. has also appointed Ms. Catherine Cai as an independent director as well as a member of the audit committee, the compensation committee, and the nominating and corporate governance committee of Lotus Technology's board of directors, with effect from March 31, 2025. Ms. Catherine Cai has more than three decades of experience in China's investment banking industry and has held senior positions at international investment banks.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Management and Board Changes 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: LOT), a leader in intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, announced significant changes to its management team and board of directors on April 8, 2025. Mr. Daxue Wang has been appointed as the new Chief Financial Officer (CFO), effective immediately, following the resignation of Mr. Alexious Lee, who will remain on the board. Mr. Wang, who previously served as CFO at Radar Auto and held executive positions at various financial institutions, is seen as a highly qualified candidate to drive financial excellence. Additionally, Ms. Catherine Cai has joined the board as an independent director, effective March 31, 2025, bringing over 30 years of experience in investment banking from her roles at UBS, Citigroup, and Bank of America Merrill Lynch. Both appointments were praised by Mr. Qingfeng Feng, CEO of Lotus, and Mr. Donghui Li, Chairman of the board, who expressed confidence that their expertise will significantly benefit the company and its shareholders as it pursues its long-term goals.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has a dedicated global team to support its R&D activities with a sizable scale and comprehensive functionalities, covering all major technological perspectives. Lotus Technology's approach to innovation demonstrates an inter-connected global collaboration among highly experienced and dedicated teams across Europe and China, each with different technological focuses. Lotus Technology's research and development efforts are focusing on the development of key EV technologies while benefiting from the technological support from the Geely Holding ecosystem. Lotus Technology intends to continually develop cutting-edge technologies, including its 800-volt EPA architecture and hardware, algorithms, and software system to enhance the competitiveness of its vehicles. Lotus Technology also plans to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing its intellectual properties and software via subscriptions to other luxury auto brands. Guided by Lotus Technology's R&D leaders, Lotus Technology's global research and development team has extensive experience in the automotive and technology industries. As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology's R&D team consisted of 1,030 professionals with extensive knowledge in automotive, engineering, software, and AI, as well as diversified working experiences from leading vehicle manufacturers globally. Such composition ensures solid technology development capabilities, especially in intelligence and digitalization. The diversified cultural and professional background promotes the exchange of ideas from different perspectives and ensures the generation of innovations. Lotus Technology established Lotus Robotics in China, the arm of its in-house intelligent driving competence. Lotus Robotics is committed to developing advanced intelligent driving technologies to foster global industry growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Worldwide Research and Development Footprint 
summary: The organization features a dedicated global team that enhances its research and development (R&D) efforts across various technological domains, with a strong emphasis on collaboration between experienced teams in Europe and China. This innovation strategy is geared towards advancing critical electric vehicle (EV) technologies, supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem. Key initiatives include the development of cutting-edge technologies like the 800-volt EPA architecture, as well as hardware, algorithms, and software systems aimed at boosting vehicle competitiveness. The organization also plans to monetize its intellectual property and software through licensing and subscription models targeted at luxury automotive brands. As of December 31, 2024, the global R&D team, led by seasoned professionals, consists of 1,030 experts in automotive engineering, software, and AI, whose diverse backgrounds contribute to enhanced technology development, particularly in intelligence and digitalization. Additionally, Lotus Robotics, established in China, is at the forefront of intelligent driving technology, providing a comprehensive suite of solutions including advanced software, cloud toolchains, and integrated development services. Since 2024, Lotus Robotics has been delivering complete intelligent driving solutions to major automakers like Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto, further solidifying its role in the global automotive industry.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Note 4   —   Earnings (Loss) per Share 
summary: The summary presents a comprehensive overview of the financial implications and leadership structure of Lotus Tech following its Business Combination. It details the calculation of earnings (loss) per share based on historical weighted average shares outstanding, adjusted for the Business Combination, which is assumed to have been effective from the beginning of the reporting period. The unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial statements for the six months ending June 30, 2023, illustrate two scenarios: one with no redemptions and another with maximum redemptions. 

Key financial findings include a pro forma net loss of $368,365 under no redemptions and $376,603 under maximum redemptions, with corresponding weighted average shares of 695,266,135 and 673,482,513, leading to loss per share figures of $0.53 and $0.56, respectively. The breakdown of shares highlights the impact of redemption assumptions on financial metrics, with detailed categories of shareholders contributing to the total share count.

Additionally, the summary outlines the pro forma financial overview for the year ending December 31, 2022, showing a net loss of $720,046 without redemptions and $724,179 with maximum redemptions, along with respective loss per share calculations of $1.16 and $1.21. 

The leadership structure post-Business Combination is also detailed, featuring key executive officers and directors, including Daniel Donghui Li (Chairman), Qingfeng Feng (CEO), and Alexious Kuen Long Lee (CFO), among others. Their backgrounds reflect extensive experience in finance, automotive, and corporate governance, underscoring the strategic leadership in place to guide Lotus Tech's future. Overall, the summary encapsulates the financial outcomes and governance framework critical to understanding Lotus Tech's position following the Business Combination.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Recently Issued Accounting Pronouncements 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Recently Issued Accounting Pronouncements and Leadership Team**

This summary provides an overview of the recently issued accounting pronouncements relevant to the company, as detailed in Note 2 of the consolidated and combined financial statements included in the prospectus. The company is classified as an emerging growth entity under the JOBS Act, reporting revenues of less than $1.235 billion for the fiscal year 2023. This classification allows the company certain exemptions that facilitate its financial reporting and compliance obligations.

**Leadership Team Overview:**

1. **Daniel Donghui Li**, 53, has served as the Chairman of the Board since November 2021. He brings extensive experience from Geely Holding Group, where he has held significant roles including Vice President, CFO, and CEO. He holds an MBA from Indiana University, along with degrees in management engineering and philosophy.

2. **Qingfeng Feng**, 51, is the Director and CEO, having been with the company since its inception. He has been part of Geely Holding since 1999, occupying various leadership positions such as Vice President and Chief Technical Officer. He holds a degree in Chemical Engineering and an Executive MBA from Tsinghua University.

3. **Alexious Kuen Long Lee**, 48, has been the CFO since the company's inception and became a director in November 2021. He has a robust background in capital access and strategy, having previously held senior roles at FIAT Industrial and CLSA.

4. **Ooi Teik Huat**, 64, joined as a director in January 2023. He has a solid background in corporate finance and has served on the boards of several public companies. He earned a Bachelor of Economics from Monash University.

5. **Ning Yu**, 53, has been a director since July 2022 and possesses 30 years of experience in the automotive industry. He is the managing partner of Zhongqing Capital and has held senior positions at Geely and Fiat. He holds a Ph.D. in Automotive Engineering.

6. **Ada Yunfeng Yan**, 54, became an Independent Director in February 2024. She has a finance background with experience at ExxonMobil and Shell and currently runs a financial consulting business. She holds a degree in international finance and an MBA.

7. **Anish Melwani**, 45, also an Independent Director since February 2024, is the Chairman and CEO of LVMH for North America. He has a strong consulting background from McKinsey & Company and holds a B.A. in Economics from Harvard University.

This summary integrates critical information regarding the company’s recent accounting pronouncements and outlines the leadership team, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements while maintaining clarity and factual accuracy. The leadership structure reflects a diverse range of expertise, supporting the company's strategic direction and operational effectiveness.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology established a nominating committee of its board of directors. The members of the nominating committee are Messrs. Litvack, Newman, and Melwani, and Mr. Litvack serves as chairman of the nominating committee. Under the Nasdaq listing standards, Lotus Technology is required to have a nominating committee composed entirely of independent directors. The board of directors has determined that each of Messrs. Litvack, Newman, and Melwani is independent. The nominating committee is responsible for overseeing the selection of persons to be nominated to serve on the board of directors of Lotus Technology. The nominating committee considers persons identified by its members, management, shareholders, investment bankers, and others.

This committee is responsible for overseeing the selection of candidates for the board, assessing individuals suggested by various stakeholders, including committee members, management, shareholders, and investment bankers.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Audit Committee 
summary: The Audit Committee is composed of three members: Ms. Ada Yunfeng Yan (chairperson), Mr. Ning Yu, and Mr. Anish Melwani. Ms. Yan is recognized as an audit committee financial expert in accordance with SEC regulations. Both Ms. Yan and Mr. Melwani satisfy the independence criteria established by Nasdaq and Rule 10A-3 of the Exchange Act, ensuring a robust governance structure.

The committee's primary responsibilities encompass a range of critical functions aimed at overseeing the company's financial integrity and compliance. These responsibilities include:

- Appointing independent auditors and pre-approving all auditing and non-auditing services to ensure objectivity and transparency.
- Reviewing audit challenges and management's responses in collaboration with independent auditors to address any discrepancies or concerns.
- Engaging in discussions regarding the annual audited financial statements with both management and auditors to ensure clarity and accuracy.
- Assessing the adequacy and effectiveness of the company's accounting and internal control policies, which includes monitoring significant financial risks that could impact the organization.
- Reviewing and approving related party transactions to prevent conflicts of interest and ensure ethical conduct.
- Conducting separate meetings with management and independent auditors to facilitate open communication and address any issues independently.
- Ensuring compliance with the company's code of business conduct and ethics, as well as reviewing the effectiveness of compliance procedures.

This structured approach to oversight is designed to enhance the company's accounting and financial reporting processes, thereby fostering accountability and transparency within the organization.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Worldwide Research and Development Footprint 
summary: Lotus has formed a dedicated global team to bolster its research and development (R&D) efforts, harnessing a wide array of technological expertise from various regions, including the U.K., Germany, and China. The company's global headquarters in Wuhan focuses on cloud computing and online data processing, while a software center in Shanghai specializes in global system integration and network security. Additionally, a research institute in Ningbo is dedicated to electric architecture, charging systems, and intelligent driving technologies. Lotus has also established two R&D centers: the Lotus Technology Creative Centre (LTCC) in Coventry, U.K., which concentrates on automotive design and sustainable materials, and the Lotus Technology Innovative Centre (LTIC) in Frankfurt, Germany, which emphasizes behavioral science and innovative vehicle technologies.

The R&D initiatives aim to advance critical electric vehicle (EV) technologies, supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem, with a particular focus on the development of the 800-volt EPA architecture and improvements in hardware, algorithms, and software systems to enhance vehicle competitiveness. Additionally, Lotus plans to monetize its R&D through licensing intellectual property and software via subscription models to other luxury automotive brands. As of December 31, 2023, the global R&D team, led by seasoned professionals, consists of 1,802 experts in automotive, engineering, software, and AI. This diverse team, drawing from leading vehicle manufacturers worldwide, fosters innovative idea exchange and enhances technology development, particularly in intelligence and digitalization.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology experienced two recent changes to its board of directors: On January 22, 2025, Abdul Farid Bin Alias was appointed as a director. He brings over 25 years of experience in investment banking, corporate finance, and capital markets, having worked with major local corporations and multinational companies including Aseambankers, Schroders, Maybank International, JPMorgan, and Khazanah. On December 6, 2024, Ooi Teik Huat resigned from his position as a director, effective immediately. His resignation was due to personal reasons and not due to any disagreements with the company regarding business, finance, accounting, or other matters.

Guided by Lotus Technology's R&D leaders, Lotus Technology's global research and development team has extensive experience in the automotive and technology industries. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology's R&D team consisted of 1,802 professionals with extensive knowledge in automotive, engineering, software, and AI, as well as diversified working experiences from leading vehicle manufacturers globally. Such composition ensures solid technology development capabilities, especially in intelligence and digitalization. The diversified cultural and professional background promotes the exchange of ideas from different perspectives and ensures the generation of innovations.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Worldwide Research and Development Footprint 
summary: Lotus has formed a dedicated global team to bolster its research and development (R&D) efforts, integrating a wide array of technological expertise across multiple locations. The team includes specialized groups in the U.K., Germany, and China, with the global headquarters in Wuhan focusing on cloud computing and data processing, a software center in Shanghai dedicated to system integration and network security, and a research institute in Ningbo that works on electric architecture, charging systems, and intelligent driving technologies. Additionally, Lotus operates two R&D centers: the Lotus Technology Creative Centre (LTCC) in Coventry, which emphasizes automotive design and sustainable materials, and the Lotus Technology Innovative Centre (LTIC) in Frankfurt, concentrating on behavioral science and innovative vehicle technologies.

Lotus's R&D initiatives prioritize the advancement of electric vehicle (EV) technologies, supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem. The company aims to innovate with cutting-edge technologies, including an 800-volt EPA architecture, while enhancing hardware, algorithms, and software systems to improve vehicle competitiveness. Furthermore, Lotus plans to leverage its R&D capabilities by licensing intellectual properties and software to other luxury automotive brands through subscription models.

As of December 31, 2023, the global R&D team, led by seasoned professionals, consists of 1,802 experts in automotive engineering, software, and AI. Their diverse experiences from leading vehicle manufacturers worldwide enrich Lotus's technology development, particularly in intelligence and digitalization, fostering a collaborative environment that drives innovation.

title: CLAWBACK POLICY

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The board of directors (the “Board”) of Lotus Technology Inc. (the “Company”) has appointed Ms. Catherine Cai as a new independent director of the Board and a member of the audit committee, compensation committee, and nominating and corporate governance committee of the Board, effective immediately. The Board has determined that Ms. Cai satisfies the independence requirements of Rule 5605(a)(2) of the Nasdaq Stock Market Rules and Rule 10A-3 under the Exchange Act. Ms. Catherine Cai has more than three decades of experience in China's investment banking industry and has held senior positions at international investment banks. She served as the Group Managing Director, Global Executive Vice Chairman, and Chairman of Greater China at UBS from 2018 to 2024. Prior to that, Ms. Cai was the Managing Director, Chairman, and Head of China at Citigroup from 2016 to 2018. Ms. Cai also held the position of Managing Director, Chairman, and Head of China Investment Banking at Bank of America Merrill Lynch. Ms. Catherine Cai obtained her bachelor’s degree in law from Peking University. Mr. Nathan Ning Yu has tendered his resignation as a member of the audit committee, compensation committee, and nominating and corporate governance committee of the Board, effective immediately. Mr. Yu will continue to serve as a director on the Board of Lotus Technology Inc. Following these changes, (i) the audit committee of the Board will be composed of Ms. Ada Yunfeng Yan, Mr. Anish Melwani, and Ms. Catherine Cai, all of whom are independent directors;

title: Changes in Board and Committee Composition 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has appointed Ms. Catherine Cai as an independent director on its board, effective immediately. She will also participate in the audit, compensation, and nominating and corporate governance committees. The board confirmed that Ms. Cai meets the independence criteria established by Nasdaq and the Exchange Act. With over 30 years of experience in China's investment banking sector, Ms. Cai has held significant positions at major international banks, including Group Managing Director at UBS and Managing Director at Citigroup. She holds a law degree from Peking University. Concurrently, Mr. Nathan Ning Yu has resigned from his roles on the audit, compensation, and nominating committees but will remain a board director. The board's committee compositions have been updated to include Ms. Cai alongside other independent directors, enhancing the governance structure.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: (ii) the compensation committee of the Board will be composed of Mr. Donghui Li, Mr. Anish Melwani, and Ms. Catherine Cai, with both Mr. Melwani and Ms. Cai being independent directors; and (iii) the nominating and corporate governance committee of the Board will be composed of Mr. Donghui Li, Ms. Ada Yunfeng Yan, and Ms. Catherine Cai, with both Ms. Yan and Ms. Cai being independent directors.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
By: /s/ Daxue Wang  
Name: Daxue Wang  
Title: Chief Financial Officer Date: June 25, 2025 Delivered close to 1,300 vehicles  
Total revenue reached \$93 million  
Gross margin was 12% as the impact from prior destocking efforts eased  
The operating loss in the first quarter narrowed by more than 50% year-on-year to \$103 million New York – June 25, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or “the Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: Daxue Wang serves as the Chief Financial Officer of Lotus Technology Inc., as indicated by his signature. In a recent report detailing the company's unaudited financial results for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025, Lotus Technology announced the delivery of nearly 1,300 vehicles and total revenue of $93 million. The company also reported an improvement in gross margin to 12%, reflecting the positive impact of previous destocking efforts. Furthermore, the operating loss for the quarter significantly decreased, narrowing by over 50% year-on-year to $103 million.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: L Catterton manages a significant number of funds. L Catterton and its affiliates, including L Catterton Asia, may compete with Lotus Technology for acquisition opportunities. If these entities decide to pursue any such opportunity, Lotus Technology may be precluded from procuring such opportunities. In addition, investment ideas generated within L Catterton may be suitable for both Lotus Technology and for current or future L Catterton funds and may be directed to such L Catterton funds rather than to Lotus Technology. Neither L Catterton nor members of Lotus Technology's management team who are also employed by L Catterton have any obligation to present Lotus Technology with any opportunity for a potential business combination of which they become aware, unless presented to such member solely in his or her capacity as an officer of Lotus Technology. L Catterton and Lotus Technology's management, in their capacities as employees of L Catterton or in their other endeavors, may be required to present potential business combinations to other entities before they present such opportunities to Lotus Technology. In addition, L Catterton or its affiliates, including L Catterton Asia, may sponsor other blank check companies similar to Lotus Technology during the period in which Lotus Technology is seeking an initial business combination, and members of Lotus Technology's management team may participate in such blank check companies. Any such companies may present additional conflicts of interest in pursuing an acquisition target, particularly in the event there is overlap among the management teams.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Conflicts of Interest 
summary: L Catterton, along with its affiliates, poses a competitive challenge for our company in securing acquisition opportunities, as they may prioritize these opportunities for their own funds and affiliates. Members of our management team, who also have roles at L Catterton, are not obligated to present potential business combinations to us unless they are acting solely in their capacity as our officers. This dual affiliation may lead to conflicts of interest, particularly if they are involved in other blank check companies that could overlap with our acquisition targets.

Under Cayman Islands law, our directors and officers are bound by fiduciary duties, including the duty of care, loyalty, good faith, disclosure, and the avoidance of conflicts of interest. These responsibilities require them to act in the best interests of the company and its shareholders, ensuring transparency and ethical governance. However, they may also have fiduciary and contractual obligations to other entities, which could limit their ability to present opportunities to us.

The management team is not required to dedicate their full time to our operations, which may further complicate the pursuit of business combinations. They are involved in multiple ventures that could create potential conflicts of interest, especially if their decisions are influenced by personal financial stakes in other companies. The company has provisions in its governing documents that allow directors and officers to engage in similar business activities, renouncing any interest in opportunities unless they are specifically offered to them in their official capacity.

Additionally, the company plans to conduct its initial business combination with the oversight of independent valuation to ensure fairness. The Sponsor and management have agreed to waive certain redemption rights and will not receive finder’s fees or consulting fees prior to the completion of the business combination. However, there remains uncertainty regarding how these conflicts will be resolved, and the outcomes may not always be favorable for our company.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Conflicts of Interest 
summary: L Catterton, along with its affiliates such as L Catterton Asia, manages multiple funds and may compete with our company for acquisition opportunities. This competition could hinder our access to certain prospects, particularly if L Catterton prioritizes its own funds or other entities over ours. Additionally, members of our management team, who are also involved with L Catterton, may face conflicts of interest when pursuing acquisition targets, especially if they are engaged with other blank check companies.

The potential for pursuing an Affiliated Joint Acquisition exists, where L Catterton and other investors may co-invest in target businesses. However, the officers and directors of our company have fiduciary duties under Cayman Islands law, which include the duty of care, loyalty, good faith, and the obligation to avoid conflicts of interest. These duties are designed to protect the interests of the company and its shareholders, ensuring responsible governance.

Despite these fiduciary responsibilities, the officers and directors may have existing commitments to other entities, which could affect their ability to prioritize opportunities for our company. The amended and restated memorandum and articles of association clarify that they are not required to refrain from engaging in similar business activities and that we waive any claims to opportunities unless specifically presented in their official capacity.

The affiliations of key individuals within L Catterton and other sectors raise concerns about potential conflicts of interest, as their multiple roles may impact their commitment to our company. Investors should be aware of these conflicts and conduct thorough due diligence. The executive officers and directors are not obligated to dedicate their full time to our company, which may further complicate their responsibilities.

The Sponsor has acquired Founder Shares and plans to purchase private placement warrants, agreeing to waive redemption rights related to the initial business combination. However, conflicts of interest may arise during the selection of a target business, especially if it involves the retention or resignation of these individuals.

To address potential conflicts, the LCAA Articles include a waiver of the corporate opportunity doctrine, allowing directors and officers to renounce interests in opportunities unless offered specifically in their capacity with LCAA. This provision aims to attract experienced leadership while ensuring that the search for business combination targets remains unaffected.

Pending necessary approvals, there are no restrictions on pursuing an initial business combination with companies affiliated with L Catterton. An independent financial fairness opinion will be sought for any such transaction to ensure its financial soundness, although this is not obligatory in all cases. The Sponsor and its affiliates will not receive finder’s fees or other compensation for services rendered prior to or during the business combination, but will be reimbursed for administrative services provided to the company. Overall, there remains uncertainty regarding the resolution of conflicts, with no guarantees of favorable outcomes.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Other than these payments and reimbursements, no compensation of any kind, including finder’s and consulting fees, will be paid by Lotus Technology to Lotus Technology's Sponsor, executive officers and directors, or their respective affiliates, prior to completion of Lotus Technology's initial business combination. After the completion of Lotus Technology's initial business combination, directors or members of Lotus Technology's management team who remain with Lotus Technology may be paid consulting or management fees from the combined company. All of these fees will be fully disclosed to shareholders, to the extent then known, in the proxy solicitation materials or tender offer materials furnished to Lotus Technology's shareholders in connection with a proposed business combination. Lotus Technology has not established any limit on the amount of such fees that may be paid by the combined company to Lotus Technology's directors or members of management. It is unlikely the amount of such compensation will be known at the time of the proposed business combination, because the directors of the post-combination business will be responsible for determining executive officer and director compensation. Any compensation to be paid to Lotus Technology's executive officers will be determined, or recommended to the board of directors for determination, either by a compensation committee constituted solely by independent directors or by a majority of the independent directors on Lotus Technology's board of directors.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Executive Compensation 
summary: The company does not provide cash compensation to its executive officers or directors. Instead, it will reimburse an affiliate of its Sponsor $10,000 monthly for office space and administrative services until the completion of its initial business combination or liquidation. Reimbursements for out-of-pocket expenses related to identifying business targets and conducting due diligence will also be made, reviewed quarterly by the audit committee, and drawn from funds outside the trust account. No additional compensation, such as finder’s or consulting fees, will be paid to the Sponsor or its affiliates until after the initial business combination. Post-combination, remaining directors or management may receive consulting or management fees, which will be disclosed to shareholders, although specific amounts may not be predetermined. There is no cap on these fees, and compensation for executive officers will be set by a compensation committee of independent directors. The company does not intend to implement measures to retain its management team after the business combination, though some executives may pursue employment or consulting roles. The company does not view management retention as crucial for selecting a target business and has no agreements providing benefits to executives upon termination.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology expects these laws and regulations to increase its legal and financial compliance costs and to render some activities more time-consuming and costly, although Lotus Technology is currently unable to estimate these costs with any degree of certainty. Many members of Lotus Technology's management team will have limited experience managing a publicly traded company, interacting with public company investors, and complying with the increasingly complex laws pertaining to public companies. Lotus Technology's management team may not successfully or efficiently manage the transition to being a public company subject to significant regulatory oversight and reporting obligations under the federal securities laws and regulations and the continuous scrutiny of securities analysts and investors. The need to establish the corporate infrastructure demanded of a public company may divert management's attention from implementing its growth strategy, which could prevent Lotus Technology from improving its business, financial condition, and results of operations. Furthermore, Lotus Technology expects these rules and regulations to make it more difficult and more expensive for the company to obtain director and officer liability insurance, and consequently, Lotus Technology may be required to incur substantial costs to maintain the same or similar coverage. These additional obligations could have a material adverse effect on Lotus Technology's business, financial condition, results of operations, and prospects. These factors could also make it more difficult for Lotus Technology to attract and retain qualified members of its board of directors, particularly to serve on Lotus Technology's audit committee, and qualified executive officers.

metadata.title_summary:
title: The requirements of being a public company may strain our resources, divert our management’s attention and affect our ability to attract and retain qualified board members. 
summary: The company faces significant challenges in complying with a range of regulatory requirements, including those set forth by the Exchange Act, Sarbanes-Oxley Act, Dodd-Frank Act, and Nasdaq listing standards. As it transitions away from its status as an "emerging growth company," it anticipates increased legal, accounting, and operational expenses, particularly due to the need for enhanced disclosure controls and internal financial reporting. This may necessitate hiring additional staff or engaging external consultants, further escalating costs.

The evolving landscape of corporate governance regulations introduces uncertainty, leading to higher compliance costs and more time-consuming processes. The ambiguous nature of these regulations allows for varying interpretations, which may change as new guidance is issued, complicating compliance efforts. While the financial impact of these changes is difficult to quantify, the management team, with limited experience in public company operations, may struggle to navigate these complexities effectively. This inexperience could hinder their ability to manage regulatory compliance and respond to investor scrutiny, potentially distracting them from growth strategies and adversely affecting the company's performance.

Moreover, the increasing regulatory demands may raise costs associated with obtaining director and officer liability insurance, further straining resources. These challenges could also impede the company's ability to attract and retain qualified board members and executive officers, particularly for critical roles like the audit committee. Additionally, the heightened visibility from required public disclosures may expose the company to litigation risks from competitors and other parties, which, even if resolved favorably, could divert valuable time and resources, ultimately harming the company's financial condition, operational results, and reputation.

metadata.title_summary:
title: We may redeem unexpired Warrants held by holders other than the Sponsor or its permitted transferees prior to their exercise at a time that is disadvantageous to the warrantholders, thereby making the Warrants worthless. 
summary: The summary outlines several critical aspects regarding the company's management of Warrants and its status as a public entity. The company retains the right to redeem Warrants before they are exercised, which could occur under conditions that may disadvantage Warrant holders, potentially rendering their investments worthless. Redemption can happen at a nominal price of $0.01 or $0.10 per Warrant, contingent on the Ordinary Shares' trading price, and requires a minimum notice period of 30 days. This situation may force Warrant holders to act against their interests, such as exercising their Warrants or selling them at unfavorable prices.

Additionally, the Warrant Agreement restricts legal actions to specific courts in New York, which may limit warrant holders' options for dispute resolution and could discourage legal action due to jurisdictional constraints. The company faces increased demands and costs associated with compliance under various securities regulations, which may divert management's focus and complicate the recruitment of qualified board members. The evolving regulatory landscape introduces uncertainty and may lead to escalating compliance costs, further straining resources.

As an "emerging growth company," the firm benefits from reduced SEC reporting requirements, but this status could diminish the attractiveness of its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) to investors. The company plans to utilize exemptions from certain reporting obligations, which may impact transparency and investor appeal, potentially resulting in a less active trading market and increased price volatility for the ADSs. Overall, these factors could adversely affect the company's financial health, operational performance, and future growth prospects.

metadata.title_summary:
title: The requirements of being a public company may strain our resources, divert our management’s attention and affect our ability to attract and retain qualified board members. 
summary: Following the Business Combination, the company will face significant compliance obligations under various regulations, including the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the Sarbanes-Oxley Act, which will result in increased legal, accounting, and operational expenses. If the company no longer qualifies as an "emerging growth company," these costs could escalate further. Key compliance requirements involve filing annual and current reports, as well as maintaining effective disclosure controls and internal financial reporting systems, potentially necessitating the hiring of additional staff or external consultants.

The evolving landscape of corporate governance and public disclosure regulations introduces uncertainty for public companies, leading to heightened compliance costs and time-consuming processes. The ambiguity in these regulations can result in varying interpretations, complicating compliance efforts and increasing the need for continuous updates to governance practices. This situation may further strain resources and escalate legal and financial expenses.

The management team may struggle with the transition to a publicly traded company due to their limited experience in navigating complex regulations and engaging with investors. This challenge could detract from their focus on growth strategies, adversely affecting the company's performance and prospects. Additionally, compliance may increase costs related to obtaining director and officer liability insurance, complicating the recruitment of qualified board members and executives, particularly for the audit committee.

Moreover, the heightened visibility from required public disclosures could expose the company to litigation threats from competitors and other parties. Even if such claims do not result in litigation or are resolved favorably, the resources spent addressing them could still negatively impact the company's financial health, operational outcomes, and reputation. Overall, these factors present ongoing challenges that could hinder the company's growth and stability in the post-Business Combination environment.

metadata.title_summary:
title: ADS holders may not be entitled to a jury trial with respect to claims arising under the deposit agreement, which could result in less favorable outcomes to the plaintiff(s) in any such action. 
summary: The deposit agreement for American Depositary Shares (ADSs) representing ordinary shares includes a stipulation that any claims arising under this agreement must be adjudicated in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York or, if jurisdiction is lacking, in New York County state courts. Notably, ADS holders waive their right to a jury trial for claims related to the deposit agreement, including those under U.S. federal securities laws. Although the enforceability of such waivers has not been definitively ruled on by the U.S. Supreme Court, they are generally considered enforceable under New York law. Potential investors are advised to consult legal counsel regarding this waiver.

The company has issued 14,541,306 warrants exercisable for ordinary shares in the form of ADSs at an exercise price of $11.50 per share, representing approximately 2.1% of the currently outstanding ordinary shares. The exercise of these warrants may lead to dilution for existing shareholders and increase the number of shares available for resale, potentially affecting market prices. The company retains the right to redeem these warrants under specific conditions, which could disadvantage warrant holders by compelling them to exercise or sell at unfavorable times. Additionally, the Warrant Agreement designates New York courts as the exclusive forum for disputes, potentially limiting warrant holders' options for legal recourse.

As a public entity, the company is subject to various reporting requirements, including compliance with the Exchange Act, Sarbanes-Oxley Act, and Dodd-Frank Act, which may increase operational costs and divert management attention. However, as an "emerging growth company," it benefits from reduced SEC reporting obligations, which may influence investor attractiveness and trading activity. The company qualifies as a foreign private issuer, exempting it from certain U.S. domestic regulations, but it risks losing this status if over 50% of its voting securities are held by U.S. holders or if certain conditions regarding its management and assets are not met.

Incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed on Nasdaq, the company is classified as a "controlled company," allowing it to adopt corporate governance practices that may differ from Nasdaq standards, potentially offering less protection to shareholders. With Mr. Shufu Li owning over 50% of the voting power, the company is exempt from having a majority of independent directors and other governance requirements. 

Investors may face challenges in enforcing U.S. court judgments against directors and officers, as many reside outside the U.S. The company's corporate governance is governed by its memorandum and articles of association, the Cayman Islands Companies Act, and common law, which may provide less protection for shareholders compared to U.S. laws. Shareholders have limited rights to inspect corporate records, which may hinder their ability to gather necessary information for shareholder actions. Consequently, public shareholders may find it more difficult to protect their interests compared to those in U.S.-incorporated companies.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: LTIC is a world-class engineering facility in electric mobility. As a part of Lotus Technology's international research and development network, LTIC develops new products and solutions for a new era of premium performance driving and participates in the development of Lotus Technology's high-end technology, such as regional development for advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), electric powertrain architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, vehicle hardware, and intelligent cabin systems. LTIC adopts an agile organizational structure where its members can work in different teams based on the focuses of various phases of the project. LTIC operates in three main areas: ● The global development team oversees global products, including, among others, digital chassis and digital vehicle dynamics. This team has assisted with component integration, durability testing, certification and homologation, as well as electric vehicle management systems. ● The regional development and delivery team collaborates with the U.K. and China teams and is responsible for global research and development platform deployment and specification development, catering to regional customer needs. This team is also responsible for ensuring compliance of the company's products with legal and regulatory requirements, including, among others, analyzing local legislation, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity matters.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Germany 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Germany Chapter: Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC)**

The Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) stands as a leading engineering facility dedicated to electric mobility, integrated within an expansive international research and development (R&D) network. The center is instrumental in advancing high-end technologies that enhance premium performance driving, focusing on key areas such as Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), Electric Power Architecture (EPA), digital vehicle dynamics, and intelligent cabin systems. LTIC's agile organizational structure fosters collaboration among team members across various project phases, promoting innovation and efficiency.

The operations at LTIC are organized into three primary areas:

1. **Global Development**: This team is responsible for the development of global products, particularly in digital chassis and vehicle dynamics. Their activities encompass component integration, durability testing, certification, and management of electric vehicle systems, ensuring that products meet high-performance standards.

2. **Regional Development and Delivery**: Collaborating closely with teams in the U.K. and China, this group focuses on deploying global R&D platforms while tailoring specifications to meet regional customer needs. They play a crucial role in ensuring compliance with legal and regulatory requirements, which includes conducting local legislation analyses, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity concerns.

3. **Innovation and New Platform Development**: This team is tasked with creating innovative concepts and architectures for future products. They explore emerging technologies and lead advancements in digital vehicle dynamics, positioning LTIC at the forefront of automotive innovation.

In addition to its operations in Germany, LTIC's R&D efforts extend to China, with significant activities concentrated in Wuhan, Shanghai, and Ningbo. These efforts focus on core technology innovations related to electric architecture, charging systems, cloud computing, data processing, system integration, network security, batteries, energy management, electric motors, and intelligent driving technologies. A notable initiative, Lotus Robotics, was established in China to enhance the company's intelligent driving capabilities. This initiative encompasses vehicle product development, software development, sensor technology, cloud and data management, testing, validation, and project management.

Overall, LTIC's commitment to electric mobility and advanced technology development positions it as a key player in the automotive industry, addressing both global and regional market demands while ensuring compliance with regulatory standards.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) is a world-class engineering facility in electric mobility. As a part of Lotus Technology's international research and development network, LTIC develops new products and solutions for a new era of premium performance driving and participates in the development of Lotus Technology's high-end technology, such as regional development for Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), Electric Powertrain Architecture (EPA), new architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, vehicle hardware, and intelligent cabin systems. Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) adopts an agile organizational structure where its members can work in different working teams based on the focuses of various phases of the project. Lotus Technology Innovation Center works in three main areas: ● The global development team oversees global products, including, among others, digital chassis and digital vehicle dynamics. This team has assisted with component integration, durability testing, certification and homologation, as well as electric vehicle management systems. ● The regional development and delivery team collaborates with Lotus Technology's U.K. and China teams and is responsible for global research and development platform deployment and specification development, catering to regional customer needs. This team is also responsible for the compliance of Lotus Technology's products with legal and regulatory requirements, including, among others, analyzing local legislation, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity matters. ● The innovation and new platform development team is responsible for creating new concepts and new architectures for Lotus Technology's future product pipeline. This team studies new technologies and pioneers in digital vehicle dynamics.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Germany 
summary: LTIC is a prominent engineering facility dedicated to electric mobility, playing a vital role in a global research and development network. The organization emphasizes the creation of innovative products and solutions that enhance premium driving performance, focusing on advanced technologies such as Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), electric powertrain architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, and intelligent cabin systems. LTIC operates with an agile structure, facilitating collaboration across various teams tailored to the specific needs of different project phases. 

The organization is divided into three main teams: the global development team, which oversees international products and focuses on digital chassis and vehicle dynamics; the regional development and delivery team, which collaborates with teams in the U.K. and China to address regional customer needs and ensure compliance with local regulations; and the innovation and new platform development team, which explores emerging technologies and develops new concepts for future products.

In parallel, China, the world's most populous country with over 1.4 billion residents, has undergone significant economic transformation, evolving into a global economic powerhouse driven by manufacturing and technology. Governed by the Communist Party, China faces challenges related to human rights and civil liberties while contributing richly to global culture and innovation. 

Within China, R&D teams in cities like Wuhan, Shanghai, and Ningbo focus on core technology innovations, including electric architecture and intelligent driving systems. Lotus Robotics, established in China, enhances this effort by specializing in intelligent driving technologies, encompassing vehicle development, software and sensor innovation, and data management. Together, these initiatives reflect China's commitment to technological advancement and its strategic role in the global economy.
